EasySaveDocs
Table of Contents

Class Logger

Namespace
EasyLog
Assembly
EasyLog.dll

Handles the generation, formatting, and dispatching of application logs. Supports writing to local files and streaming to a remote centralized logging server via TCP sockets. Implements the Singleton pattern to ensure a single logging stream across the application.

public class Logger
Inheritance
object
Logger

Methods

formatCompleteSaveMessage(string, string, string, string)

Formats the summary data of a fully completed backup job into a dictionary structure suitable for logging.

public static Dictionary<string, string> formatCompleteSaveMessage(string name, string source, string target, string time)

Parameters

name string

The name of the backup job.

source string

The root source directory.

target string

The root target directory.

time string

The timestamp of completion.

Returns

Dictionary<string, string>

A dictionary containing the structured data.

formatErrMessage(string)

Formats an application exception or error message into a dictionary structure suitable for logging.

public static Dictionary<string, string> formatErrMessage(string errorMessage)

Parameters

errorMessage string

The raw error string or exception message.

Returns

Dictionary<string, string>

A dictionary containing the error string and a timestamp.

formatInfoRealTimeMessage(string, string, string, string, dynamic)

Formats the raw data of a real-time state change into a dictionary structure suitable for logging.

public static Dictionary<string, string> formatInfoRealTimeMessage(string name, string source, string target, string time, dynamic saveTaskState)

Parameters

name string

The name of the backup job.

source string

The source file path.

target string

The target file path.

time string

The timestamp of the event.

saveTaskState dynamic

The dynamic state of the task (e.g., RUNNING, PAUSED).

Returns

Dictionary<string, string>

A dictionary containing the structured data.

formatLogMessage(string, string, string, int, double, string)

Formats the raw data of a completed file transfer into a dictionary structure suitable for logging.

public static Dictionary<string, string> formatLogMessage(string name, string source, string target, int size, double transferTime, string time)

Parameters

name string

The name of the backup job.

source string

The source file path.

target string

The target file path.

size int

The size of the transferred file in bytes.

transferTime double

The time taken to copy the file in milliseconds.

time string

The timestamp of the completion.

Returns

Dictionary<string, string>

A dictionary containing the structured data.

getCurrentFormat()

Gets the current LogFormat enum value being used by the logger.

public LogFormat getCurrentFormat()

Returns

LogFormat

The active log format.

getInstance(IConfig)

Retrieves the thread-safe singleton instance of the Logger.

public static Logger getInstance(IConfig config)

Parameters

config IConfig

The configuration provider to use if the logger needs to be initialized.

Returns

Logger

The single active instance of the Logger.

getLogsFormat()

Retrieves the current format as a string (useful for UI data binding).

public string getLogsFormat()

Returns

string

A string representation of the format (e.g., "Json" or "Xml").

isConnectedToServer()

Checks whether the logger currently has an active TCP connection to the remote server.

public bool isConnectedToServer()

Returns

bool

true if the socket is connected; otherwise, false.

log(Dictionary<string, string>)

Records a historical log entry. It appends the log to a local daily file and/or sends it to the remote server, depending on the active configuration settings.

public bool log(Dictionary<string, string> message)

Parameters

message Dictionary<string, string>

A dictionary containing the key-value pairs representing the log data.

Returns

bool

true if the log was successfully recorded/sent everywhere it was supposed to go; otherwise, false.

logRealTime(Dictionary<string, string>)

Records a real-time log entry by completely overwriting the target file. This is used to maintain a constant "current state" of the application for external monitoring tools.

public bool logRealTime(Dictionary<string, string> message)

Parameters

message Dictionary<string, string>

A dictionary containing the state data.

Returns

bool

true if the file was successfully overwritten; otherwise, false.

reconnectToServer()

Forces a disconnection and attempts to establish a new connection to the remote logging server.

public bool reconnectToServer()

Returns

bool

true if the reconnection was successful; otherwise, false.

setCurrentFormat(LogFormat)

Sets the LogFormat enum and persists the change to the global configuration file.

public void setCurrentFormat(LogFormat format)

Parameters

format LogFormat

The new LogFormat to apply.

setLogsFormat(string)

Attempts to parse a string input into a LogFormat enum and updates the logger.

public void setLogsFormat(string format)

Parameters

format string

A string representing the format ("json" or "xml", case-insensitive).
